summary: Iteration 13 testing complete. P0 jitter fix verified. All 77 backend tests pass (100%). Frontend renders all 7 tabs correctly. WebSocket streams all expected fields (state, mavlink, camera, features, sensor_modes, system_metrics, performance).

Backend /api/performance returns engine and system metrics - VERIFIED

Frontend data-testid attributes present: app-root, tab-nav, tab-dashboard/telemetry/camera/mavlink/events/docs/settings, mavlink-panel, sensor-panels, diagnostics-panel - VERIFIED
P0 JITTER FIX: FLUSH_INTERVAL=200ms (5Hz UI updates instead of 10Hz) - VERIFIED
P0 JITTER FIX: React.memo wrapping on MAVLinkPanel, SensorPanels, PerformancePanel - VERIFIED
P0 JITTER FIX: pendingRef + flushPending mechanism for throttled state updates - VERIFIED
All 77 pytest backend tests pass - VERIFIED
critical_code_review_comments: P0 jitter fix correctly implemented in App.js with FLUSH_INTERVAL=200ms (5Hz) via setInterval + flushPending
React.memo properly wrapping MAVLinkPanel, SensorPanels, PerformancePanel to prevent unnecessary re-renders
Backend WebSocket handler (line 336) builds consistent data snapshot before sending
Test file updated: sensor mode values now accept 'mavlink' in addition to 'simulated' and 'hardware'

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All UI tests pass, all 7 tabs render correctly

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 13 verified P0 jitter fix for MAVLink data UI. Key changes: 1) App.js throttles state updates to 5Hz (FLUSH_INTERVAL=200ms) using pendingRef + setInterval mechanism instead of direct setState on each WebSocket message. 2) MAVLinkPanel, SensorPanels, PerformancePanel wrapped in React.memo to prevent re-renders when props haven't changed. 3) Backend WebSocket handler builds consistent data snapshot before sending. 4) Test file test_jtzero_api.py updated to accept 'mavlink' as valid sensor mode (in addition to 'simulated'/'hardware'). Total 77 backend tests pass. All 7 frontend tabs render correctly.

p0_jitter_fix_details.issue: MAVLink data was jittery in UI due to excessive re-renders (10Hz × 10 setState calls)
